free_text: Monoenergetic photons were produced by the
technique of fast-positron annihilation in flight.
The neutron detector consisted of a 61-cm cube
of paraffin in which were embeded forty-eight
proportional counters. The detector efficiency is ~40
(1.2)% for a typical prompt fission neutron spectrum
and ~45(1.3)% for a typical delayed neutron spectrum.
each ring of BF3 counters are presented to an on-line
computing system. The on-line data acquisition system
information obtained following each beam burst. In
addition, the individual ring totals in coincidence
with one-, two-, and three-fold events as well as the
ring totals for all events were recorded. These data
totals were used to determine neutron spectral
data were acquired by counting neutron events between
beam bursts at times such that entire prompt neutron
